seo_title: Innovation Characterization of Hallucination in Large Language Models
meta_description: Researchers introduce innovation as a property that measures the tendency of a model to produce outputs outside the training data, and show its relation to…
body_html:
The property of a calibrated LLM that makes hallucinations unavoidable is innovation. Innovation measures the tendency of a model to produce outputs outside the training data, and it is implied by the condition for hallucination identified by Kalai and Vempala.

Key FindingsInnovation is an almost characterization of hallucination: hallucination implies innovation, and conversely, innovation implies hallucination with high probability.The

source.title: Innovation: An Almost Characterization of Hallucination
source.summary: Researchers have introduced a concept called "innovation" as a measure of how likely large language models (LLMs) are to produce outputs outside their training data. Innovation is related to the condition for hallucination identified by previous work, implying that it is an almost characterization of hallucination. In other words, if innovation is high, hallucinations will occur with high probability, and conversely, if hallucinations occur, innovation is likely to be present. This new concept provides a simpler way to understand and mitigate the limitations of LLMs.
